title: Infinite Difficulty Tiers
problem: Floor scaling stops at floor 10 with no endgame. A 3-tier system (Normal/Nightmare/Hell) has a hard ceiling. Need infinite tiers so gear acquired at any point has a harder tier to push into, and the designer can add godly gear without a cap.
analysis: gameStore has floor (1–10) with no tier concept. maxHpForFloor and buildPlayerStats scale linearly with floor only. Monster scaling in combat.ts uses floor multiplier only. The plan's 3-tier model (Normal/Nightmare/Hell) creates a hard ceiling — infinite numeric tiers remove that ceiling and make gear perpetually relevant as the designer can always introduce harder content.
implementation: 1. gameStore: `tier: number` (starts 1, persists across deaths). `careerStats.tiersCleared: number[]` tracks first-time tier completions. `careerStats.ascensions: number` counts prestige loops. `firstTimeTierClear: number | null` in-memory flag set by nextFloor, read by GridScreen, cleared by clearFirstTimeTierClear(). 2. nextFloor(): when floor >= 10, detects isFirstClear = !tiersCleared.includes(completedTier), sets firstTimeTierClear, appends to tiersCleared, resets floor to 1, increments tier. 3. endRun(): preserves tiersCleared and ascensions in updatedCareerStats across every run. 4. ascendRun(): increments careerStats.ascensions, pre-loads ghostCharm from last graveyard entry, resets floor/hp/mana to floor 1 values. 5. Monster stat multiplier: hpMult = 1 + (tier-1) * 0.5, damageMult = 1 + (tier-1) * 0.35 — applied in combatStore startCombat. 6. Loot quality bonus MF: (tier-1) * 20 added to magicFind for loot rolls. 7. HUD: shows T{tier}·F{floor} badge with optional boss skull. 8. gridStore.initFloor uses tier in seed: seed ^ (floor * 0x1337) ^ (tier * 99991). 9. TierClearOverlay: enhanced with isFirstTime prop — shows '✦ FIRST CLEAR ✦' eyebrow and extra gold label on first-time clears. 10. MainMenu: ascension badge '✦×N' shown when careerStats.ascensions > 0. 11. Victory screen: ASCEND button shown when graveyard has entries; triggers ascendRun() and navigates to classSelect.

decisions.decision: Infinite numeric tiers instead of named Normal/Nightmare/Hell
decisions.rationale: No hard ceiling means the designer can always introduce harder content. Named tiers imply finality; numbers imply an open ladder.
alternativesConsidered: Prestige system that resets gear on tier unlock

decisions.decision: Tier persists across deaths; floor resets to 1 on death within a tier
decisions.rationale: Death is a floor-level setback, not a tier-level setback. Losing a tier would feel too punishing given the graveyard sacrifice also occurs.
alternativesConsidered: Death resets both tier and floor

decisions.rationale: Additive scaling prevents absurd numbers at high tiers while keeping each tier noticeably harder. Tier 5 monsters have 3x HP — challenging but not impossible with tier-appropriate gear.

decisions.decision: Loot MF bonus: (tier-1)*20 added to magicFind for all loot rolls
decisions.rationale: Ensures higher tiers are always more rewarding in quality, not just harder. The gear treadmill needs a carrot, not just a stick.
alternativesConsidered: Flat loot table per tier (new item pool)
No loot quality boost — only difficulty increases

decisions.decision: Track first-time tier clears in careerStats.tiersCleared: number[]
decisions.rationale: Allows the TierClearOverlay to distinguish a first-time clear from a repeat. First clears get a special '✦ FIRST CLEAR ✦' eyebrow for recognition without permanently changing the flow.
alternativesConsidered: Boolean flag per tier in a map

decisions.decision: Ascension mechanic: ASCEND button on victory screen pre-loads Ghost Echo from last graveyard entry
decisions.rationale: Ascension is a deliberate prestige-style restart that keeps tier progress, increments an ascension counter, and rewards the player with a Ghost Echo head-start — making the loop feel rewarding rather than punitive.
alternativesConsidered: Ascension just restarts with no bonus

successCriteria: Clearing floor 10 increments tier and resets to floor 1. Monster HP and damage scale correctly per tier formula. HUD shows both tier and floor. Loot improves at higher tiers. Death resets floor to 1 but not tier. First-time tier clears show the '✦ FIRST CLEAR ✦' variant of TierClearOverlay. careerStats.tiersCleared accumulates correctly and persists. Ascension button available on victory screen when graveyard is non-empty; ascendRun() increments ascensions and pre-loads Ghost Echo.
knownLimitations: No tier-specific monster pool — same monsters appear at all tiers, just scaled
No visual theme change per tier in v1
Very high tiers (20+) untested for balance

The in-game tier formula (1+(tier-1)×0.5 HP / 1+(tier-1)×0.35 dmg) is calibrated for infinite progression; the sim-tested Normal/Nightmare/Hell named-difficulty model uses a separate decoupled formula (see applyTierScaling in monsters.ts)
recommendations: Add tier to GraveyardEntry — dying on Tier 7 should be noted. Tier number could eventually unlock themed monster sets or unique floor modifiers. The sim-validated Named Difficulty Tier model (Normal/Nightmare/Hell with decoupled HP/dmg multipliers — see applyTierScaling in monsters.ts) is the intended replacement for the linear per-tier formula in a future difficulty redesign.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: BMAD session 2: add tiersCleared[], ascensions, firstTimeTierClear, ascendRun(), first-clear TierClearOverlay variant, ASCEND button, ascension badge — all implemented and verified

revisions.author: claude+marc
revisions.reason: BMAD S6: document applyTierScaling() (Normal/Nightmare/Hell decoupled scaling) in monsters.ts — sim-designed, 800-player validated, all 3 classes BALANCED. Separate from in-game linear tier formula; intended for future named-difficulty implementation.

publicInterface.name: tier
publicInterface.description: Current difficulty tier, starts at 1, increments on floor-10 clear, persists across deaths

publicInterface.name: firstTimeTierClear
publicInterface.description: Set to the completed tier number when cleared for the first time; GridScreen reads it to show the special overlay variant, then calls clearFirstTimeTierClear()

publicInterface.name: careerStats.tiersCleared
publicInterface.description: Array of tier numbers that have been cleared at least once; drives first-time detection

publicInterface.name: careerStats.ascensions
publicInterface.description: How many times the player has used the ASCEND mechanic (prestige restarts)

publicInterface.name: clearFirstTimeTierClear
publicInterface.description: Clears firstTimeTierClear back to null after the overlay has been dismissed

publicInterface.name: ascendRun
publicInterface.description: Prestige-restart: increments ascensions, pre-loads Ghost Echo from last graveyard entry, resets to floor 1

publicInterface.name: tierMonsterMultiplier
publicInterface.description: Returns stat multipliers for monsters at the given tier (used in combatStore) — linear formula: hp=1+(tier-1)×0.5, dmg=1+(tier-1)×0.35

publicInterface.name: applyTierScaling
publicInterface.description: Decoupled named-difficulty scaler used by fullRunSim and designed for future in-game NM/Hell implementation. diffTier 1=Normal (no scaling), 2=Nightmare (2.5×HP/1.80×dmg), 3=Hell (3.1×HP/2.0×dmg). Exported from src/engine/monsters.ts.

codeSnippets.snippet:
<Text style={styles.eyebrow}>

codeSnippets.label: Victory screen ASCEND button

userGuide:
**Tiers** are the infinite difficulty ladder of this game. When you clear floor 10, you ascend to the next tier — the HUD badge (e.g. T2·F7) always shows where you stand.

**Monster scaling:** Each tier multiplies monster HP by `1 + (tier-1) × 0.5` and damage by `1 + (tier-1) × 0.35`. Tier 2 monsters have 150% HP and 135% damage; Tier 5 monsters have 300% HP and 240% damage. There is no ceiling.

**Loot bonus:** Each tier above 1 adds `(tier-1) × 20` to your Magic Find for every loot roll — higher tiers always drop better gear.

**Death:** Resets your floor to 1 but never your tier. You always fight at the difficulty you have earned.

**First clears:** The very first time you clear a tier, the TIER COMPLETE overlay shows a special gold '✦ FIRST CLEAR ✦' banner. Repeat clears show a standard overlay.

**Ascension:** On the victory screen, if your graveyard has items, you can press ASCEND instead of returning to the menu. Ascending increments your ascension count (shown as ✦×N on the main menu), starts a new run at the same tier and level, and pre-loads a Ghost Echo from your most recently sacrificed item — giving you a spectral head-start.

codexEntry.title: Difficulty Tiers
codexEntry.summary: Clear floor 10 to advance to the next tier. Tiers increase forever — harder fights, better loot.
mechanics: Tiers advance by clearing floor 10. There is no cap.
Monster HP multiplier per tier: 1 + (tier−1) × 0.5  (Tier 2 = 1.5×, Tier 5 = 3×)
Monster damage multiplier per tier: 1 + (tier−1) × 0.35  (Tier 2 = 1.35×, Tier 5 = 2.4×)
Loot Magic Find bonus: (tier−1) × 20 added automatically to all drops
Tier persists across deaths — you always fight at the difficulty you have earned
Death resets floor to 1 but never reduces your tier
HUD shows your current position as T{tier}·F{floor}
tips: A tier-clear overlay appears when you ascend — read it to see the new monster scaling before descending.
Higher tiers are required to unlock better unique items (each unique has a minimum floor requirement that must be met at the right tier).
Tier 1 is meant to be completed with low-quality gear. By Tier 3 you should be stacking resists.

implementationNotes: The in-game linear tier formula (1+(tier-1)×0.5 HP, 1+(tier-1)×0.35 dmg) in combatStore is intentionally separate from applyTierScaling in monsters.ts — the former is an infinite-progression system, the latter is the sim-validated Normal/Nightmare/Hell named-difficulty system wired into the game via combatStore startCombat via gs.tier.
firstTimeTierClear is in-memory only (not persisted) — if the app is killed during the overlay it won't re-appear, which is acceptable since the graveyard sacrifice overlay also has this limitation.
The tier seed mixing (seed ^ (tier * 99991)) in gridStore.initFloor ensures that the same floor in different tiers generates completely different room layouts without adding a separate RNG dimension.
TIER_BG for T2 is blue-tinted (#0d0d10) not green, matching Nightmare's blue theme — originally shipped as green (#0d100d) which conflicted with the difficulty color system.
